best.description: wider MLP + OneCycleLR + MSE+L1 loss for both MLP and CFP

what_worked: Wider MLP layers (more capacity for 900-dim output)
MSE+L1 combined loss (L1 matches sparse target distribution)
what_consistently_failed: Activation changes (GELU, SiLU both terrible, ReLU is optimal)
Sigmoid output (amazing SSIM but always hurts RMSE)
Wider CFP channels (MLP quality is the bottleneck, not CFP capacity)
Weight decay (not needed with existing BatchNorm+Dropout)
interesting_tradeoffs: Sigmoid gives SSIM 0.82-0.85 but RMSE 0.053+ (fundamental RMSE/SSIM tension)
Huber loss consistently boosts SSIM at RMSE cost
L1 weight 0.2 gives better SSIM but slightly worse RMSE than 0.1

notes: 43 experiments, 5 kept. Model is well-optimized. Next session could explore: different data augmentation, ensemble methods, or completely different architectures.
